task_context.description: This is a detector design and performance characterization task for high-energy physics instrumentation. The user wants to design a compact hadronic calorimeter specifically for measuring hadronic particle energies in a mid-energy collider experiment environment. The task involves exploring different detector geometries and materials through Monte Carlo simulation, then evaluating the detector's performance across multiple metrics including energy resolution, response linearity, and shower containment efficiency. The approach requires systematic testing of up to 3 different detector configurations using Geant4 simulations with hadronic particle beams at multiple energy points to assess how well the calorimeter can measure and contain hadronic showers.
task_context.goal: Design and optimize a compact hadronic calorimeter capable of accurately measuring hadronic particle energies at a mid-energy collider experiment
constraints: Simplified experimental environment with no magnetic field
Energy sweep capped at 50 GeV maximum
Limited to 3 energy points for simulation tractability
Must use pi+ or protons in Geant4 particle gun
